alt_text: Two side-by-side cards: left card on parchment background labelled 'BITE' with the Arabic word عَجَمَۃ; right card on blue background labelled 'CUT' with the Arabic word عَرَبَۃ.
notes: Etymology lesson contrasting 'ajama (foreign/inarticulate, 'biting' on words) with 'araba (Arab/clearly-speaking, 'cutting' clean syllables) — basis for understanding why Arabic is called clear/cutting speech.
